channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Japanese court convicts Australian who says she was tricked into smuggling drugs
item.description: CHIBA, Japan (AP) — A Japanese court on Wednesday sentenced an Australian woman who says she was tricked amphetamines into the country to six years in prison, despite accepting her testimony that she was the victim of an online romance scam.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Activists file second impeachment complaint against embattled Philippine Vice President Duterte
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — A group led by left-wing activists filed a second impeachment complaint against the Philippine vice president on Wednesday over her alleged misuse of government funds and demanded that she be permanently barred from holding public office.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about South Korea and its short-lived but chaotic declaration of martial law
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — South Korea's President Yoon Suk Yeol faced parliamentary moves to impeach him after he sent heavily armed forces into Seoul's streets with his baffling and sudden declaration of martial law that harkened to the country’s past dictatorships.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Israeli strike kills a person in Lebanon, further shaking the tenuous ceasefire
item.description: NABATIYEH, Lebanon (AP) — Israeli forces carried out several new drone and artillery strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday, including a deadly strike that the Health Ministry and state media said killed one person, further shaking a tenuous ceasefire meant to end more than a year of fighting with Hezbollah.
item.dc:creator: BASSAM HATOUM and SALLY ABOU ALJOUD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Shock martial law edict in South Korea follows chaotic recent history: A look at the crucial context
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — It may seem that the wild predawn scenes in Seoul, with hundreds of armed troops and frantic lawmakers storming South Korea's parliament building after the president suddenly declared martial law, came out of nowhere.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean leader facing mounting calls to resign or be impeached over martial law
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol is facing pressure from legislators and the public to step down or be impeached after he ended a martial law.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian insurgents capture four central towns as government forces reclaim some territory
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Syrian insurgents captured four new towns early Tuesday, bringing them closer to the central city of Hama, opposition activists said, while government forces retook some territory they lost last week.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Senegalese artisans in the spotlight as they exhibit for the first time at a prestigious art event
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — For the artistic and cultural elites of Senegal, the monthlong Dakar Biennale of Contemporary African Arts is a celebratory moment.
item.dc:creator: MONIKA PRONCZUK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power shortages in Ecuador are melting away the future of a small town’s ice-cream industry
item.description: SALCEDO, Ecuador (AP) — Ice-cream production in Salcedo, a quaint town in Ecuador's central highlands, began in the mid-20th century, born from the ingenuity of Franciscan nuns.
item.dc:creator: GONZALO SOLANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Floods wreak havoc in Malaysia, southern Thailand with over 30 killed, tens of thousands displaced
item.description: KOTA BARU, Malaysia (AP) — Severe floods caused by monsoon rains killed more than 30 people and displaced tens of thousands in Malaysia and southern Thailand, officials said Tuesday, with both countries preparing shelters and evacuation plans in anticipation of more heavy rain.
item.dc:creator: SYAWALLUDIN ZAIN and JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea’s opposition parties submit a motion to impeach President Yoon over sudden martial law
item.description: South Korea’s opposition parties have submitted a motion to impeach President Yoon Suk Yeol, who is facing pressure to leave office hours after he ended a short-lived martial law that prompted troops to encircle parliament before lawmakers voted to lift it.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: South Korea lifts president's martial law decree after lawmakers reject military rule
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — The president of South Korea early Wednesday lifted the martial law he imposed on the country hours earlier, bending to political pressure after a tense night in which troops surrounded parliament and lawmakers voted to reject military rule.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN human rights watchdog opens investigation into Venezuela presidential election
item.description: MIAMI (AP) — The top United Nations human rights watchdog on Tuesday ordered Venezuela to avoid destroying tally sheets and other electoral material as it investigates allegations that President Nicolás Maduro stole this summer's election. The U.N.
item.dc:creator: JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Namibia will have its first female leader after VP wins presidential election for the ruling party
item.description: WINDHOEK, Namibia (AP) — Namibia elected its first female leader as Vice President Netumbo Nandi-Ndaitwah was declared the winner Tuesday of a presidential election last week that was tarnished by technical glitches that caused a three-day extension to allow votes to be cast, and rejected as illegal
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes on Gaza hospital wound 3, Netanyahu vows 'iron fist' in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli drone strikes hit Kamal Adwan Hospital on Tuesday, wounding three medical staff at one of the few hospitals still partially operating in the northernmost part of Gaza, the facility’s director said. Dr. Hossam Abu Safiya said the drones were dropping bombs, spraying shrapnel at the hospital.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: UN watchdog to conduct probe into sexual misconduct allegations against top international prosecutor
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — A United Nations watchdog has been selected to lead an external probe into allegations of sexual misconduct against the top prosecutor of the International Criminal Court, The Associated Press learned Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL and JOSHUA GOODMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A landmark climate change case opens at the top UN court as island nations fear rising seas
item.description: THE HAGUE, Netherlands (AP) — The top United Nations court took up the largest case in its history on Monday, hearing the plight of several small island nations helpless in combating the devastating impact of climate change that they feel endangers their very survival.
item.dc:creator: MOLLY QUELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vanuatu calls on ICJ to recognize climate change's harm
item.description: Representatives for the island nation of Vanuatu called on the International Court of Justice this week to recognize the harm caused by climate change, the first remarks the United Nations court heard as it considers international obligations to address climate impacts.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, The Hill

item.title: Guinea stadium stampede kills 56 following clashes at soccer match
item.description: Reports said security had tried to use tear gas to restore calm after the chaos that followed a disputed penalty call.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US Navy ships repel attack from Houthis in Gulf of Aden
item.description: The U.S. Navy destroyers USS Stockdale and USS O’Kane repelled an attack against American ships by Yemen-based Houthi rebels, CENTCOM says.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Largest gold deposit in the world worth $83 billion found in China
item.description: A discovery of gold reserves, estimated to be worth $83 billion, was made in China last month. China is the world's largest gold producer, Reuters reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tiffany Trump's father-in-law Massad Boulos to become senior presidential adviser on Arab affairs
item.description: ​President-elect Trump on Sunday said he would appoint Massad Boulos, a billionaire from Lebanon whose son Michael is married to Tiffany Trump, to serve as his senior adviser on Arab and Middle Eastern affairs.
item.dc:creator: Ian Swanson, The Hill

item.title: Ireland election: Incumbent center-right parties likely to retain power, but left-wing Sinn Fein holds water
item.description: Ireland's election results have yet to be finalized, but exit polling suggests a coalition government between the center-right Fine Gael and Fianna Fáil.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former NATO chief says Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire a 'direct result' of incoming Trump admin
item.description: James Stavridis, former NATO supreme allied commander, said he believes the Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire agreement was successful in part because of President-elect Trump’s impending return to power. “Iran [is] very vulnerable.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Islamist rebels in Syria catch Assad, Putin, Iran regimes off guard giving US new mideast headache
item.description: Amid a rapid-fire collapse of Syrian, Hezbollah, Russian and Iranian forces in northern Syria, questions are surfacing about U.S. policy toward the emerging new order in the war-ravaged Syrian Arab Republic.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Imprisoned Kremlin critic convicted again, receives 3-year sentence for opposing war in Ukraine
item.description: Imprisoned Kremlin critic Alexei Gorinov was convicted again on Friday and sentenced to three years in prison for opposing Russia's ongoing war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Canadian news publishers sue OpenAI over alleged copyright infringement
item.description: OTTAWA, Ontario (AP) — A coalition of Canadian news publishers, including The Canadian Press, Torstar, Globe and Mail, Postmedia and CBC/Radio-Canada, has filed a lawsuit against OpenAI for using news content to train its ChatGPT generative artificial intelligence system.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Missing hiker survived 50 days in Canadian wilderness, at times in frigid temps
item.description: A 20-year-old hiker who had been missing for weeks was found alive in the wilderness of British Columbia, the Royal Canadian Mountain Police confirmed on Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Unger, NewsNation

item.title: Millions in Ukraine without power after Russia attacks grid
item.description: “Attacks on energy facilities are happening all over Ukraine,” Energy Minister Herman Halushchenko said on Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Trump taps retired General Keith Kellogg for Ukraine envoy role
item.description: While there is currently no special envoy for the war in Ukraine, Trump had privately expressed interest in creating the position.
item.dc:creator: Gram Slattery, Svea Herbst-Bayliss and Steve Holland / Reuters, NewsNation
